summary.intent: Implement a browser-in-browser feature for the moltis agent platform, allowing users to view and interact with live browser sessions from the settings UI, with session history, cookie persistence, and per-agent browser profiles.
summary.outcome: Fully functional browser viewer with live CDP screencast, mouse/keyboard/scroll interaction, URL autocomplete, per-agent cookie isolation, session history with action logs in SQLite, and a scrollbar overlay. 40+ commits landed on PR #531.

repo: serde `#[serde(default)]` only handles *missing* JSON keys — explicit `null` still fails deserialization for non-Option types like u64. Null-stripping before deserialization is required when LLMs send null for every optional field.
BrowserTool and RealBrowserService each created independent BrowserManager instances; sessions created by one were invisible to the other. Fixed via shared Arc<OnceCell<BrowserManager>>.

The WebSocket broadcast layer (broadcast.rs) has a subscription filter — clients must explicitly subscribe to event types or events are silently dropped. `browser.screencast.frame` was missing from websocket.js subscription list.

CDP Page.startScreencast relay task exited when tx.send() found 0 receivers instead of dropping frames — causing a race where the first frame arrived before any subscriber, permanently killing the relay.
axum 0.8 changed path parameter syntax from `:param` to `{param}` — panics at startup otherwise.
Browser viewport was 2560x1440@2x in user's moltis.toml config, overriding code defaults. CDP SetDeviceMetricsOverride on screencast start is the right fix to resize for the viewer.
CDP mouseWheel event requires both deltaX and deltaY always — omitting either when zero causes a CDP error.
Screencast metadata offset_top must be added to y-coordinates for click accuracy; image naturalWidth/naturalHeight are more reliable than hardcoded dimensions for scale factors.
Canvas event listeners attached via useEffect([]) never fire when canvas is conditionally rendered — must use a ref callback instead.
System biome (2.4.6) and npx biome (0.3.3) produce different formatting output; local-validate.sh uses system biome.

code.finding: Null-stripping via obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) placed after sandbox injection, before deserialization.
Relay loop must use LazyFrame drop (continue) not exit when no subscribers. First-frame race condition root cause.
subscribeEvents list missing 'browser.screencast.frame' — every frame was silently filtered. This was the primary 'Waiting for first frame' bug.
Added windowsVirtualKeyCode mapping for special keys (Backspace=8, Enter=13, arrows=37-40, etc.) — Chrome ignores key events without VK codes.
All sandbox sessions share ~/.moltis/browser/profile/sandbox/{profile_id}/ — SingletonLock must be removed before each launch. Profile routing: UI→'default', agents→'session_{key}'.
RealBrowserService uses OnceCell for session store to allow wiring after db_pool is created (which is initialized later in server startup).
axum route used :session_id syntax, must be {session_id} in axum 0.8.

workflow: Run local-validate.sh with PR number to validate before pushing; biome format must be applied with the system biome binary not npx biome.
YubiKey-gated git push requires user interaction; background tasks should retry after user confirmation rather than looping.
Integration tests for CDP interactions require --test-threads=1 to avoid profile lock conflicts.
CDP Input.insertText is the correct way to paste clipboard text into a focused element in a remote browser.
When adding new events to the gateway broadcast system, always add them to the WebSocket subscription list in websocket.js or they will be silently dropped.

friction: YubiKey tap required for every git push — interrupted flow repeatedly throughout the session.
Biome version mismatch (npx 0.3.3 vs system 2.4.6) caused multiple rounds of CI failures even after running --write locally.
User's moltis.toml had explicit viewport overrides that masked the code default change — required inspecting config file to diagnose.
serde null vs missing behavior was a non-obvious surprise requiring understanding of serde internals.
The screencast 'Waiting for first frame' issue required multiple debugging rounds across 3 separate root causes (null deserialization, relay exit, missing WS subscription).
axum 0.8 breaking path syntax change caused a startup panic not caught by compile-time checks.
CDP mouseWheel silently failed when deltaX was omitted — no error logged, scroll just didn't work.
open_items: WebRTC upgrade (neko architecture) for sub-100ms latency and 6-18x bandwidth reduction vs CDP JPEG screencast.
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast to eliminate ~33% base64 encoding overhead.
Session recording as video — action log exists but no video capture yet.
Playwright e2e tests for browser interaction (click accuracy, scroll) are written but require a real browser runtime to execute.
Multiple concurrent sandbox sessions may have cookie conflicts since they share one profile directory — needs investigation for agent use cases.
History page shows action logs but no screenshots for closed sessions.
Password manager integration for the browser viewer is explicitly deferred as too complex.
